notes: Forward returns at 5/20/60d horizons overlap across consecutive daily signals; effective independent sample is far below nominal n. Directional evidence only — not grounds for mechanical parameter changes.
v8 telemetry (divergence, hostile_review, regime driver/basis) began accruing mid-June 2026; sections 2-4 deepen with time.
Temporal z baseline excludes the most recent observation; generate-signals consumes the stored baseline next morning (one-day staleness, immaterial at window 60).

tactical.falsifier: A close back above the 50-day SMA ($304.95) with RSI reclaiming 50+ would confirm the drawdown has bottomed and flip this to a stronger buy; conversely a break below the 52w low $250.02 would flip it toward neutral/trim as the trend accelerates down.

positional.falsifier: Next earnings showing book-to-bill <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or 90d EPS revisions turning down >1%, would flip this to trim; a B/B >1.10 with upward EPS revisions would justify pushing back toward -60.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that forward P/E is at a >10% discount to cohort with margins recovering toward 13%+ would flip this negative (buy); conversely a NDAA/CR shock signaling sequester risk or margins deteriorating below 9% on structural causes would push firmly to trim.

tactical.falsifier: A single-day drop >4% on non-fundamental news (buy signal) OR RSI spiking >75 into a vertical blowoff (trim signal) would flip the tactical sign.

positional.falsifier: A close below the SMA50 ($1667) on volume, breaking the uptrend structure, would flip positional to a caution/trim posture.

strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E climbing above 45x (via earnings cut or further price surge) OR TSMC+Samsung+Intel all signaling capex cuts would push strategic firmly positive/trim; conversely a >15% drawdown with backlog intact flips it negative/buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sustained break below the 50-day SMA ($511.76) on rising volume would flip this to trim/positive; a decisive bounce back toward $540+ would flip it more negative.

positional.falsifier: LIN breaking below its 200-day ($470.48) or the LIN/APD spread inverting sharply (LIN underperforming APD by >2pp) would flip the sign positive.

strategic.falsifier: A confirmed structural ROCE decline (clean operating ROCE, ex-goodwill, dropping below 20% with price/mix ex-FX turning negative) would validate real moat erosion and push the score higher; conversely a >$1B H2 mega-project announcement or backlog-accelerating earnings beat flips it negative.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking back above 55 with price reclaiming the SMA50 (~$405) on Azure/earnings strength would flip this to a clean momentum buy; conversely a break below the $349 52w low on heavy volume flips it toward neutral/sell as the downtrend confirms.

positional.falsifier: Reclaiming the SMA50 (~$405) and turning the MA structure toward a golden-cross path would confirm the positional buy; a decisive break of the $349.20 52w low with Azure CC decelerating below 22% would flip the sign toward trim.

strategic.falsifier: A sign flip requires forward P/E pushing above 35x WITH Azure CC decelerating below 22% and OpenAI partnership governance/IP deterioration — a combination of premium multiple and thesis impairment; alternatively an accommodative rate shift (real rate <0.5%) plus Azure reacceleration would deepen the buy.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp RSI move below 35 (oversold on a rate-driven selloff) would flip this to a buy; a spike above 70 on no fundamental change would flip it to a trim.

positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps (via a 10Y drop toward 3.5% or an ENB price selloff) would flip this to a buy; a dividend-coverage scare would push it more aggressively to trim.

strategic.falsifier: Confirmed dividend-growth guidance cut or a structural Mainline volume/regulatory impairment would push the score sharply positive; onset of a rate-cut cycle with the yield spread widening past 200bps would flip it negative (buy).

tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below the 200-day SMA ($12.60) on rising volume would flip this from a buyable dip to a broken-uptrend trim, pushing the tactical score positive.

positional.falsifier: GLNCY/COPX spread turning materially negative (GLNCY lagging copper by >0.5pp) combined with a completed death cross would signal the market is discounting the trading arm and mining together — flipping positional toward neutral/positive.

strategic.falsifier: A sustained break of LME copper below ~$8,000/t accompanied by rising visible inventories and confirmation that Chinese base-metal demand has structurally rolled over would invalidate the cyclical-trough buy thesis and flip strategic positive.

tactical.falsifier: RSI dropping below 30 would strengthen the buy (more negative); a break below $32.84 with rising volume would flip tactical positive as support fails.

positional.falsifier: Sustained net ETF inflows (multi-week positive) AND a reclaim of the 50DMA/200DMA would confirm regime change and push the score more negative; conversely a decisive break below $32.84 on accelerating outflows would flip me toward neutral/positive as the cycle-broken thesis confirms.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the halving cycle has structurally broken (e.g., BTC failing to make a new all-time high this cycle combined with sustained ETF net outflows over multiple quarters) would push strategic positive; accelerating sovereign/institutional adoption would push it negative.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive break below the 200-day SMA (~$98) on volume, or an RSI drop below 30, would flip this to a buy setup on oversold; conversely a surge above the 50-day with momentum would tilt slightly negative.

positional.falsifier: MXN/USD breaking above ~18.5 (peso weakening) sustained would flip me positive/trim; a break toward 16.5 (peso strength) would flip me negative/add.

strategic.falsifier: A Fed pivot driving real rates toward 0.5% combined with confirmed forward PE below 50x on intact cRPO would flip strategic to a clear buy.
